nodes.label: check-overflow.mjs mobile viewport overflow QA script
nodes.norm_label: check-overflow.mjs mobile viewport overflow qa script

nodes.label: Primary color: black (design decision)
nodes.rationale: Client brief and July 2026 client feedback both mandate black/cool palette; warm palette experiments were tried and rejected twice, so friendliness is delivered via shape/type/motion instead of color.

nodes.label: Never introduce warm/cream/brown tints rule
nodes.rationale: Guards against regressing the black/cool palette decision; explicitly bans ivory backgrounds, warm grays, warm-tinted shadows anywhere in the design system.

nodes.label: Rounded geometry design system (pills, rounded-2xl)
nodes.rationale: Delivers the client-requested comfort/friendliness without touching the black color system: pill buttons, rounded-2xl cards, no sharp corners, no mono/uppercase UI labels.

nodes.label: CSS-first calm motion system (rise keyframes, Reveal, header-scroll-fx)
nodes.rationale: No animation libraries; motion carries the 'friendliness' the client wants, respects prefers-reduced-motion.
nodes.norm_label: css-first calm motion system (rise keyframes, reveal, header-scroll-fx)

nodes.label: Never use em dash or en dash in public copy
nodes.rationale: Copy style constraint; enforced mechanically by scripts/check-copy-style.mjs as part of the standard copy QA gate before delivery.
nodes.norm_label: never use em dash or en dash in public copy

nodes.label: NAP single source of truth (src/lib/site-config.ts)
nodes.rationale: Prevents inconsistent name/address/phone across the site by centralizing it in one config module, never hardcoded elsewhere.

nodes.label: Bilingual NL (canonical) / EN (/en/) routing
nodes.rationale: Dutch URLs are canonical per site-architecture.md; English lives under /en/ with translated slugs via next-intl pathnames.

nodes.label: Strict B2B positioning vs Airco@home consumer brand
nodes.rationale: Site must never compete with the commercial CTA; consumer brand only gets a small, visually secondary residential redirect.
nodes.norm_label: strict b2b positioning vs airco@home consumer brand

nodes.label: Lead Form -> GoHighLevel webhook flow
nodes.rationale: POST /api/lead re-validates with zod then forwards to GHL_WEBHOOK_URL with sourcePage/locale/submittedAt for CRM attribution; degrades to server log if unset.

nodes.label: Location pages deferred to SEO Phase 4
nodes.rationale: City pages (e.g. /zakelijke-airco-voorschoten/) are only built after core service pages are stable, to avoid diluting the core commercial cluster and thin/duplicate local content.
nodes.norm_label: location pages deferred to seo phase 4

nodes.label: "This is NOT the Next.js you know" breaking-changes notice
nodes.rationale: Warns that this Next.js version diverges from training data conventions; explicitly calls out middleware.ts having been renamed to proxy.ts.
nodes.norm_label: "this is not the next.js you know" breaking-changes notice

nodes.label: Fast + mobile-first performance rule (Lighthouse mobile >=90)
nodes.rationale: Business visitors browse on the go; mandates next/image, lean JS, Lighthouse mobile >=90 / SEO 100.

nodes.label: JSON-LD schema requirement (Organization/LocalBusiness/WebSite/ContactPoint/Service/BreadcrumbList/FAQPage)
nodes.rationale: Structured data was entirely absent on the old site (Rich Results Test showed none detected); FAQPage only added when the FAQ is visibly rendered.

nodes.label: Preferred anchor-text list / forbidden vague anchors
nodes.rationale: Bans 'Klik hier'/'Lees meer'/'Meer informatie'/'Bekijk dit'; mandates descriptive keyword-bearing anchors for internal linking SEO value.
nodes.norm_label: preferred anchor-text list / forbidden vague anchors

nodes.label: Dutch URLs verbatim with trailing slash (trailingSlash: true)
nodes.rationale: Client-approved final URL structure must never be invented or renamed; trailingSlash true in next.config.ts is the technical enforcement mechanism.
nodes.norm_label: dutch urls verbatim with trailing slash (trailingslash: true)

nodes.label: CTA label per page type table
nodes.rationale: Maps page type (property/installation/system/maintenance/contract/repair) to one fixed CTA label to keep conversion intent unambiguous.
nodes.norm_label: cta label per page type table

nodes.label: Homepage 17-section flow
nodes.rationale: Distilled and expanded from the client's indicative 9-section homepage structure in ProjectInformation.md into a fully specified 17-section build order.

nodes.label: Universal 7-section service page template
nodes.rationale: Caps every service page at 7 content sections (Hero, Overview, Need&Benefits, Scope, Process, Proof, Conversion) to keep pages short and consistent per AGENTS.md rule 8.

nodes.label: Lead Form field spec (company, contact, phone, email, location, property type, service, message)
nodes.rationale: Defines required/optional fields plus preselected service/property-type per page, and the source-page CRM-tracking requirement.
nodes.norm_label: lead form field spec (company, contact, phone, email, location, property type, service, message)

nodes.label: CID-based Google Maps embed avoids unnamed pin
nodes.norm_label: cid-based google maps embed avoids unnamed pin
